value.description: User-facing interaction (requests, elicitation, UAT)
Planning and design (proposals, reviews, ratification)
Implementation (slices, code review, landing)
All review criteria satisfied; no BLOCKER items
BLOCKER issues found; must provide actionable feedback
Security, type errors, test failures, broken production code paths
value.label: aura:severity:blocker

value.description: Performance, missing validation, architectural concerns
value.label: aura:severity:important

value.description: Style, optional optimizations, naming improvements
value.label: aura:severity:minor

value.description: Must complete before next step starts
Can run concurrently with sibling steps in same parallel-group
Full inline context with all decisions and rationale
Summary with Beads task ID references
Change is isolated to a single file
Change spans a module or package
Change affects multiple modules or subsystems
Straightforward implementation, familiar patterns
Some design decisions needed, moderate scope
Significant design work, unfamiliar territory, or many moving parts
No external API changes, no breaking changes
Introduces new public interfaces or APIs
Modifies existing behavior or public contracts
Well-known patterns, team has done this before
Unfamiliar domain, requires research and exploration
Familiar domain, low complexity — brief prior art check (local only)
Moderate complexity or some novelty — find existing patterns and standards (local + docs)
High complexity, new territory, or high risk — thorough domain analysis (local + web)

key-questions.q: Does the implementation faithfully serve the user's original request?
Are technical decisions consistent with the rationale in the proposal?
Are there gaps where the proposal says one thing but the code does another?
Favour integration tests over brittle unit tests?
System under test NOT mocked — mock dependencies only?
Shared fixtures for common test values?
Assert observable outcomes, not internal state?
Design the API you know you will need?
No under-engineering (cutting corners on security or correctness)?
Complexity proportional to innate problem complexity?

phase.name: Request
phase.description: Capture, classify, research, and explore user request

substep.description: Classify request along 4 axes: scope, complexity, risk, domain novelty
Find domain standards, prior art, relevant documentation

transition.condition: classification confirmed, research and explore complete

phase.name: Elicit
phase.description: User Requirements Elicitation survey and URD creation

substep.description: URE survey: structured Q&A with user to capture requirements
Create URD as single source of truth for requirements

task-title.convention: Scoping URE to determine which IMPORTANT/MINOR findings to address
Requirements doc for follow-up scope. References original URD.

substep.description: Full technical proposal: interfaces, approach, validation checklist, BDD criteria

task-title.convention: N increments per revision. Old proposals marked aura:superseded.
Proposal accounting for original URD + FOLLOWUP_URD + outstanding findings

substep.description: Each reviewer assesses one axis (A/B/C). All 3 must ACCEPT.

severity-tree.reason: Plan reviews use binary ACCEPT/REVISE only

phase.name: Plan UAT
phase.description: User acceptance test on the plan

substep.description: Present plan to user with demonstrative examples. User approves or requests changes.

phase.name: Ratify
phase.description: Ratify the accepted proposal, supersede old ones

substep.description: Add ratify label. Mark prior proposals aura:superseded. Create placeholder IMPL_PLAN.

same-actor-as.note: Architect performs p5, p6, p7 — no handoff between them

substep.description: Create handoff document with full inline provenance. Transfer to supervisor.

skill-invocation.directive: Supervisor launch prompt MUST start with Skill(/aura:supervisor)

phase.name: Impl Plan
phase.description: Supervisor decomposes ratified plan into vertical slices

substep.description: Identify production code paths. Create SLICE-N tasks with leaf tasks. Assign workers.

step.instruction: Call Skill(/aura:supervisor) to load role instructions
Read RATIFIED_PLAN and URD via bd show
Spawn ephemeral Explore subagents via Task tool for scoped codebase queries
step.context: Each subagent is short-lived and returns findings; no standing team overhead
Vertical slices give one worker end-to-end ownership of a feature path (types → tests → impl → wiring) with clear file boundaries

step.instruction: Create leaf tasks (L1/L2/L3) for every slice

transition.condition: all slices created with leaf tasks, assigned, and dependency-chained

substep.description: Each worker owns full vertical: types, tests, implementation, wiring

task-title.convention: N identifies slice within the implementation plan
Follow-up slice. Adopts IMPORTANT/MINOR leaf tasks from original review as children (dual-parent: leaf blocks both original severity group AND follow-up slice).

transition.condition: all slices complete, quality gates pass

layer.name: Tests
layer.description: Tests importing production code (will fail initially)

layer.name: Implementation
layer.description: Make tests pass. Wire with real dependencies. No TODOs.

substep.description: Each reviewer reviews ALL slices against their axis. EAGER severity tree.

transition.condition: all 3 reviewers ACCEPT, all BLOCKERs resolved

transition.action: fix BLOCKERs in affected slices, then re-review

severity-tree.rule: Always create 3 severity groups per review round, even if empty.
Empty groups have no children and are closed immediately.

followup-epic.trigger: review-completion AND (IMPORTANT OR MINOR findings exist)

phase.name: Impl UAT
phase.description: User acceptance test on the implementation

substep.description: Present implementation to user. User approves or requests fixes.

transition.action: create fix tasks in affected slices

phase.name: Landing
phase.description: Commit, push, close tasks, hand off

substep.description: git agent-commit, bd sync, git push. Close upstream tasks.

transition.condition: git push succeeds, all tasks closed or dependency-resolved

role.name: Epoch
role.tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Skill, Agent, Task
role.introduction: You are the master orchestrator for the full 12-phase epoch lifecycle. You delegate planning phases (1-7) to the architect and implementation phases (7-12) to the supervisor.
role.ownership-narrative: You own the full 12-phase lifecycle from Request to Landing. You delegate phases 1-7 to the architect and phases 7-12 to the supervisor. The epoch role coordinates the complete workflow end-to-end and is the only role that spans all phases.

role.name: Architect
role.label-awareness: aura:p1-user, aura:p2-user, aura:p3-plan, aura:p4-plan, aura:p5-user, aura:p6-plan, aura:p7-plan
role.tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Skill, Agent, Task
role.introduction: You design specifications and coordinate the planning phases of epochs. See the project's AGENTS.md and ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md for coding standards and constraints.
role.ownership-narrative: You own Phases 1-7 of the epoch: capture and classify user request (p1), run requirements elicitation URE survey (p2), create PROPOSAL-N with full technical plan (p3), spawn 3 axis-specific reviewers and loop until consensus (p4), present plan to user for acceptance test (p5), add ratify label to accepted PROPOSAL-N (p6), create handoff document and transfer to supervisor (p7).

behavior.then: use BDD Given/When/Then format with acceptance criteria
spawn 3 axis-specific reviewers (A=Correctness, B=Test quality, C=Elegance)

behavior.should-not: close or delete the proposal task

role.name: Reviewer
role.description: End-user alignment reviewer for plans and code
role.introduction: You review from an end-user alignment perspective. See the project's protocol/CONSTRAINTS.md for coding standards.
role.ownership-narrative: You participate in two phases: Phase 4 (plan review) — evaluate PROPOSAL-N against one axis using binary ACCEPT/REVISE, NO severity tree; Phase 10 (code review) — review ALL implementation slices against your axis using full severity tree (BLOCKER/IMPORTANT/MINOR), EAGER creation of all 3 severity groups.

behavior.then: vote REVISE with specific actionable feedback

role.name: Supervisor
role.description: Task coordinator, spawns workers, manages parallel execution
role.label-awareness: aura:p7-plan, aura:p8-impl, aura:p9-impl, aura:p10-impl, aura:p11-user, aura:p12-impl, aura:epic-followup

invariants.invariant: NEVER implements code — always spawns workers
NEVER explores codebase directly — delegates to ephemeral Explore subagents
ALWAYS creates leaf tasks within each slice — no undecomposed slices
Creates follow-up epic when code review has IMPORTANT or MINOR findings

role.tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Skill, Agent, Task
role.introduction: You coordinate parallel task execution. See the project's AGENTS.md and ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md for coding standards and constraints.
role.ownership-narrative: You own Phases 7-12 of the epoch: receive handoff from architect (p7), create vertical slice decomposition IMPL_PLAN (p8), spawn workers for parallel implementation SLICE-N (p9), spawn reviewers for per-slice code review with severity tree (p10), coordinate user acceptance test (p11), commit, push, and hand off (p12). You NEVER implement code directly — all implementation is delegated to workers.

behavior.then: read ratified plan, URD, UAT, and elicit tasks for full context

behavior.given: trivial changes (single-file edits, config tweaks, typo fixes)
behavior.then: use model: haiku to minimize cost and latency
behavior.should-not: use a heavyweight model for trivial work

behavior.then: prefer model: sonnet for the Task tool to ensure quality
behavior.should-not: default to haiku for complex work

behavior.then: follow the Ride the Wave cycle: plan tasks with integration points, launch the wave of workers, spawn reviewers for per-slice review (clean exit = 0 BLOCKERs + 0 IMPORTANTs), workers fix per-slice with atomic commits, max 3 cycles per slice, escalate to architect after cycle 3
behavior.should-not: skip any stage; batch review across slices; exceed 3 review cycles per slice

role.name: Worker
role.description: Vertical slice implementer (full production code path)
role.tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Skill, Edit, Write
role.ownership-model: One worker per production code path. Owns full vertical (types → tests → implementation → wiring).
role.introduction: You own a vertical slice (full production code path from CLI/API entry point → service → types). See the project's AGENTS.md and ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md for coding standards and constraints.
role.ownership-narrative: NOT: A single file or horizontal layer (e.g., 'all types' or 'all tests'). YES: A full vertical slice (complete production code path end-to-end). You own the FEATURE end-to-end, not a layer or file. Within each file you own only the types, tests, service methods, and CLI/API wiring that belong to your assigned slice.

behavior.then: own full production code path (types → tests → impl → wiring)
plan backwards from end point to types
behavior.should-not: start with types without knowing the end

behavior.then: import actual production code (CLI/API users will run)
behavior.should-not: create test-only export or dual code paths

behavior.then: manually trace the production code path end-to-end (entry point → service → types) to confirm wiring, error handling, and no dead code — beyond what automated gates check
behavior.should-not: treat passing tests as sufficient verification without a manual walkthrough

command.name: aura:epoch
command.description: Master orchestrator for full 12-phase workflow

command.name: aura:plan
command.description: Plan coordination across phases 1-6

command.name: aura:status
command.description: Project status and monitoring via Beads queries

command.name: aura:user:request
command.description: Capture user feature request verbatim (Phase 1)

command.name: aura:user:elicit
command.description: User Requirements Elicitation survey (Phase 2)

command.name: aura:user:uat
command.description: User Acceptance Testing with demonstrative examples

command.name: aura:architect
command.description: Specification writer and implementation designer

command.name: aura:architect:propose-plan
command.description: Create PROPOSAL-N task with full technical plan

command.name: aura:architect:request-review
command.description: Spawn 3 axis-specific reviewers (A/B/C)

command.name: aura:architect:ratify
command.description: Ratify proposal, mark old proposals aura:superseded

command.name: aura:architect:handoff
command.description: Create handoff document and transfer to supervisor

command.name: aura:supervisor
command.description: Task coordinator, spawns workers, manages parallel execution

command.name: aura:supervisor:plan-tasks
command.description: Decompose ratified plan into vertical slices (SLICE-N)

command.name: aura:supervisor:spawn-worker
command.description: Launch a worker agent for an assigned slice

command.name: aura:supervisor:track-progress
command.description: Monitor worker status via Beads

command.name: aura:supervisor:commit
command.description: Atomic commit per completed layer/slice

command.name: aura:worker
command.description: Vertical slice implementer (full production code path)

command.name: aura:worker:implement
command.description: Implement assigned vertical slice following TDD layers

command.name: aura:worker:complete
command.description: Signal slice completion after quality gates pass

command.name: aura:worker:blocked
command.description: Report a blocker to supervisor via Beads

command.name: aura:reviewer
command.description: End-user alignment reviewer for plans and code

command.name: aura:reviewer:review-plan
command.description: Evaluate proposal against one axis (binary ACCEPT/REVISE)

command.name: aura:reviewer:review-code
command.description: Review implementation slices with EAGER severity tree

command.name: aura:reviewer:comment
command.description: Leave structured review comment via Beads

command.name: aura:reviewer:vote
command.description: Cast ACCEPT or REVISE vote (binary only)

command.name: aura:impl:slice
command.description: Vertical slice assignment and tracking

command.name: aura:impl:review
command.description: Code review coordination across all slices (Phase 10)

command.name: aura:msg:send
command.description: Send a message to another agent via Beads comment

command.name: aura:msg:receive
command.description: Check inbox for messages from other agents

command.name: aura:msg:broadcast
command.description: Broadcast a message to multiple agents

command.name: aura:msg:ack
command.description: Acknowledge received messages

command.name: aura:explore
command.description: Codebase exploration — find integration points, existing patterns, and related code
command.note: Used in Phase 1 (s1_3) by architect, and in Phase 8 by supervisor's ephemeral Explore subagents.

command.name: aura:research
command.description: Domain research — find standards, prior art, and competing approaches

command.name: aura:test
command.description: Run tests using BDD patterns

command.name: aura:feedback
command.description: Leave structured feedback on any Beads task

handoff.required-fields: request, urd, proposal, ratified-plan, context, key-decisions, open-items, acceptance-criteria

skill-invocation.note: Supervisor launch prompt MUST start with this invocation. Without it, supervisor skips leaf task creation.

handoff.required-fields: request, urd, proposal, ratified-plan, impl-plan, slice, context, key-decisions, open-items, acceptance-criteria

skill-invocation.note: Worker message MUST include explicit instruction to call this skill.

handoff.required-fields: request, urd, proposal, ratified-plan, impl-plan, context, key-decisions, acceptance-criteria

skill-invocation.note: Reviewer prompt MUST include instruction to call this skill.

handoff.required-fields: request, urd, impl-plan, slice, context, key-decisions, open-items
request, urd, proposal, context, key-decisions, open-items, acceptance-criteria
handoff.note: Reviewer hands IMPORTANT/MINOR findings to supervisor, who creates the follow-up epic

handoff.required-fields: request, urd, followup-epic, followup-ure, followup-urd, context, key-decisions, findings-summary, acceptance-criteria
handoff.note: Follow-up specific. Supervisor completes FOLLOWUP_URE and FOLLOWUP_URD, then hands off to architect with scoped findings and requirements for FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL creation.

constraint.then: add labels and comments only
constraint.should-not: delete or close tasks prematurely, remove labels

constraint.then: chain dependency: bd dep add parent --blocked-by child
constraint.should-not: skip dependency chaining or invert direction

example.label: correct
example.code: # Full dependency chain: work flows bottom-up, closure flows top-down bd dep add request-id --blocked-by ure-id bd dep add ure-id --blocked-by proposal-id bd dep add proposal-id --blocked-by impl-plan-id bd dep add impl-plan-id --blocked-by slice-1-id bd dep add slice-1-id --blocked-by leaf-task-a-id

constraint.then: all 3 reviewers must ACCEPT before proceeding
constraint.should-not: proceed with any REVISE vote outstanding

constraint.then: use ACCEPT or REVISE only
constraint.should-not: use APPROVE, APPROVE_WITH_COMMENTS, REQUEST_CHANGES, or REJECT

constraint.then: ALWAYS create 3 severity group tasks (BLOCKER, IMPORTANT, MINOR) immediately
constraint.should-not: lazily create severity groups only when findings exist

example.label: correct
example.code:
# Create all 3 severity groups immediately (even if empty) bd create --title "SLICE-1-REVIEW-A-1 BLOCKER" \ --labels "aura:severity:blocker,aura:p10-impl:s10-review" bd create --title "SLICE-1-REVIEW-A-1 IMPORTANT" \ --labels "aura:severity:important,aura:p10-impl:s10-review" bd create --title "SLICE-1-REVIEW-A-1 MINOR" \ --labels "aura:severity:minor,aura:p10-impl:s10-review"
# Close empty groups immediately bd close <empty-important-id> bd close <empty-minor-id>

example.label: anti-pattern
example.code: # WRONG: only creating groups when findings exist # This skips empty groups and breaks the audit trail if blocker_findings: bd create --title "BLOCKER" ...

constraint.then: use binary ACCEPT/REVISE only
constraint.should-not: create severity tree for plan reviews

constraint.given: a BLOCKER finding in code review
constraint.then: add as child of BOTH the severity group AND the slice it blocks
constraint.should-not: add to severity group only

constraint.given: code review completion with IMPORTANT or MINOR findings
constraint.then: create immediately upon review completion
constraint.should-not: gate follow-up epic on BLOCKER resolution

constraint.then: each production code path owned by exactly ONE worker (full vertical)
constraint.should-not: assign horizontal layers or same path to multiple workers

constraint.then: spawn workers for all code changes
constraint.should-not: implement code directly

constraint.then: spawn ephemeral Explore subagents via Task tool for scoped codebase queries; each subagent is short-lived and returns findings; no standing team overhead
constraint.should-not: explore the codebase directly as supervisor; maintain a standing explore team

constraint.then: clean review exit requires 0 BLOCKERs AND 0 IMPORTANTs; MINORs are acceptable and tracked in FOLLOWUP epic; each slice has its own independent review cycle counter (max 3 cycles); after 3 failed cycles, escalate to architect for re-planning
constraint.should-not: accept review with open BLOCKERs or IMPORTANTs; batch review across multiple slices; exceed 3 cycles without escalating; escalate to user instead of architect

constraint.then: 4 user-gated phases only: (1) research depth decision, (2) URE survey, (3) Plan UAT, (4) Impl UAT; all other phase transitions are auto-ratified by the supervisor; after Plan UAT ACCEPT, proceed directly to ratification without user gate
constraint.should-not: add additional user gates beyond the 4 defined; require user approval for ratification after UAT ACCEPT

constraint.given: multiple vertical slices share types, interfaces, or data flows
constraint.then: identify horizontal Layer Integration Points and document them in IMPL_PLAN; each integration point specifies: owning slice, consuming slices, shared contract, merge timing; include integration points in slice descriptions so workers know what to export and import
constraint.should-not: leave cross-slice dependencies implicit; assume workers will discover contracts on their own

constraint.then: workers notify supervisor with bd comments add (not bd close); slices must be reviewed at least once by reviewers before closure; only the supervisor closes slices, after review passes
constraint.should-not: close slices immediately upon worker completion; allow workers to close their own slices

constraint.then: limit to a maximum of 3 cycles per slice; clean review exit = 0 BLOCKERs + 0 IMPORTANTs; after cycle 3, escalate to architect for re-planning if BLOCKERs or IMPORTANTs remain; remaining IMPORTANT findings move to FOLLOWUP epic
constraint.should-not: exceed 3 review cycles per slice; escalate to user instead of architect; batch review across multiple slices

constraint.then: create Beads leaf tasks (L1: types, L2: tests, L3: impl) within each slice with bd dep add slice-id --blocked-by leaf-task-id
constraint.should-not: create slices without leaf tasks — a slice with no children is undecomposed and cannot be tracked

constraint.given: an agent is launched for a new phase (especially p7 to p8 handoff)
constraint.then: prompt MUST start with Skill(/aura:{role}) invocation directive so the agent loads its role instructions
constraint.should-not: launch agents without skill invocation — they skip role-critical procedures like ephemeral exploration and leaf task creation

constraint.then: parent blocked-by child: bd dep add stays-open --blocked-by must-finish-first
constraint.should-not: invert (child blocked-by parent)

constraint.then: use description frontmatter references: block
constraint.should-not: use bd dep relate (buggy) or blocking dependencies for reference docs

constraint.then: use git agent-commit -m ...
constraint.should-not: use git commit -m ...

constraint.then: title PROPOSAL-{N} where N increments; mark old as aura:superseded
constraint.should-not: reuse N or delete old proposals

constraint.then: title {SCOPE}-REVIEW-{axis}-{round} where axis=A|B|C, round starts at 1
constraint.should-not: use numeric reviewer IDs (1/2/3) instead of axis letters

constraint.given: user interview (Request, URE, or UAT), URD update, or mid-implementation design decision
constraint.then: capture full question text, ALL option descriptions, AND user's verbatim response; the URD is the living document of ALL user requests, URE, UAT, and mid-implementation design decisions and feedback — update it via bd comments add whenever user intent is captured
constraint.should-not: summarize options as (1)/(2)/(3) without option text, or paraphrase user responses

example.label: correct
example.code: # Full question, all options with descriptions, verbatim response bd create --title "UAT: Plan acceptance for feature-X" \ --description "## Component: Verbose fields **Question:** Which verbose fields are useful? **Options:** - backupDir (full path): Shows where the backup landed - session ID: Enables log correlation across events - repo path + hash: Confirms which git repo was detected **User response:** backupDir (full path), session ID **Decision:** ACCEPT"

example.label: anti-pattern
example.code: # WRONG: options summarized as numbers, response paraphrased bd create --title "UAT: Plan acceptance" \ --description "Asked about verbose fields (1-4). User picked 1 and 2. Accepted."

constraint.then: run same protocol phases with FOLLOWUP_* prefix: FOLLOWUP_URE → FOLLOWUP_URD → FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL → FOLLOWUP_IMPL_PLAN → FOLLOWUP_SLICE
constraint.should-not: skip the follow-up lifecycle or treat the follow-up epic as a flat task list

constraint.when: assigning original IMPORTANT/MINOR leaf tasks to follow-up slices
constraint.then: add leaf task as child of follow-up slice (dual-parent: leaf blocks both severity group AND follow-up slice)
constraint.should-not: remove the leaf task from its original severity group parent

constraint.then: run quality gates (typecheck + tests) AND verify production code path (no TODOs, real deps)
constraint.should-not: close with only 'tests pass' as completion gate

constraint.given: an error, exception, or user-facing message
constraint.then: make it actionable: describe (1) what went wrong, (2) why it happened, (3) where it failed (file location, module, or function), (4) when it failed (step, operation, or timestamp), (5) what it means for the caller, and (6) how to fix it
constraint.should-not: raise generic or opaque error messages (e.g. 'invalid input', 'operation failed') that don't guide the user toward resolution

title-convention.note: N increments per revision. Old proposals marked aura:superseded.
N identifies slice within the implementation plan
Follow-up epic created after code review with IMPORTANT/MINOR findings. Single-parent epic relationship — no followup-of-followup.
Scoping URE to determine which IMPORTANT/MINOR findings to address
Requirements doc for follow-up scope. References original URD.
Proposal accounting for original URD + FOLLOWUP_URD + outstanding findings
Follow-up slice. Adopts IMPORTANT/MINOR leaf tasks from original review as children (dual-parent: leaf blocks both original severity group AND follow-up slice).

document.purpose: Protocol entry point and quick-start guide
Core agent directive: philosophy, constraints, roles, label schema
Coding standards, checklists, severity definitions, naming conventions
Step-by-step workflow execution (single source of truth)
Role taxonomy: phases owned, tools, handoffs per agent
Command reference: all /aura:* skills mapped to phase and role
Label and title migration from v1 to v2
User Acceptance Test structured output template
This file: canonical machine-readable protocol definition (BCNF)

entity.note: Single source of truth for all entity definitions and relationships

document.purpose: Project README with workflow overview, commands, structure
Agent orchestration guide for this repository

dependency-model.rule: Parent (stays open) is blocked-by child (must finish first). Work flows bottom-up; closure flows top-down.
dependency-model.canonical-chain: REQUEST → blocked-by ELICIT → blocked-by PROPOSAL → blocked-by IMPL_PLAN → blocked-by SLICE-N → blocked-by leaf tasks

reference-links.note: URD and other reference docs use frontmatter, not blocking deps

followup-lifecycle.trigger: Code review completion AND (IMPORTANT OR MINOR findings exist)

dependency-chain.note: Same protocol phases but with FOLLOWUP_ prefix

step.description: Epic created by supervisor. References original URD and review tasks.
Scoping URE with user to determine which findings to address.
Requirements doc for follow-up scope. References original URD.
Proposal accounting for original URD + FOLLOWUP_URD + outstanding findings.
Each slice adopts original IMPORTANT/MINOR leaf tasks as children.

leaf-task-adoption.rule: When supervisor creates FOLLOWUP_SLICE-N, the IMPORTANT/MINOR leaf tasks from the original review gain a second parent: the follow-up slice. This is the same dual-parent pattern as BLOCKER findings.
leaf-task-adoption.command: bd dep add {followup-slice-id} --blocked-by {important-leaf-task-id} bd dep add {followup-slice-id} --blocked-by {minor-leaf-task-id}
leaf-task-adoption.note: Leaf tasks retain their original parent (the severity group from the original review) AND gain the follow-up slice as a second parent. Both must close for the leaf to be fully resolved.

ref.note: Follow-up epic references original URD via frontmatter
Follow-up epic references review tasks via frontmatter

handoff-chain.note: How handoffs flow through the follow-up lifecycle

transition.description: Reviewer → Followup: Bridge from original review to follow-up epic. Created by supervisor when IMPORTANT/MINOR findings exist. This handoff STARTS the follow-up lifecycle.
Supervisor creates FOLLOWUP_URE (same actor — supervisor owns follow-up epic and initiates scoping)
Supervisor creates FOLLOWUP_URD (same actor within Phase 2 — supervisor synthesizes follow-up requirements)
Supervisor → Architect: Hands off completed FOLLOWUP_URE + FOLLOWUP_URD to architect for FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL creation. Architect receives scoped findings and requirements.
Architect → Supervisor: After FOLLOWUP_PROPOSAL is ratified, architect hands off to supervisor for FOLLOWUP_IMPL_PLAN. Handoff doc references original URD, FOLLOWUP_URD, and outstanding findings.
Supervisor → Worker: FOLLOWUP_SLICE-N assignment. Worker receives both the follow-up slice spec AND the original leaf task IDs they must resolve.
Supervisor → Reviewer: Code review of follow-up slices. Reviewer receives follow-up context + original findings being addressed.
Worker → Reviewer: Worker completes follow-up slice. Handoff includes which original leaf tasks were resolved.

step.instruction: Call Skill(/aura:supervisor) to load role instructions
Read RATIFIED_PLAN and URD via bd show
Spawn ephemeral Explore subagents via Task tool for scoped codebase queries
step.context: Each subagent is short-lived and returns findings; no standing team overhead
Vertical slices give one worker end-to-end ownership of a feature path (types → tests → impl → wiring) with clear file boundaries

step.instruction: Create leaf tasks (L1/L2/L3) for every slice

example.label: template
example.code: bd create --labels aura:p9-impl:s9-slice \ --title "SLICE-1-L1: Types -- <slice name>" \ --description "--- references: slice: <slice-1-id> impl_plan: <impl-plan-task-id> urd: <urd-task-id> --- Layer 1: types and interfaces for <slice name>."

step.instruction: Tests importing production code (will fail initially)
Make tests pass. Wire with real dependencies. No TODOs.

checklist.item: All 3 reviewers ACCEPT, no open BLOCKERs
FOLLOWUP epic created if any IMPORTANT/MINOR findings exist
All upstream tasks closed or dependency-resolved
Can only close on a review wave, not a worker wave
Eligible to close only after review by independent agents with no BLOCKERS or IMPORTANT findings
All workers have notified completion via bd comments add
Ephemeral reviewers spawned for all slices
Severity groups (BLOCKER/IMPORTANT/MINOR) eagerly created per slice
No TODO placeholders in CLI/API actions
Real dependencies wired (not mocks in production code)
Tests import production code (not test-only export)
No dual-export anti-pattern (one code path for tests and production)
Production code path verified end-to-end via code inspection
Supervisor notified via bd comments add (not bd close)
Can only close on a review wave, not a worker wave
Eligible to close only after review by independent agents with no BLOCKERS or IMPORTANT findings

coord-cmd.template: bd update <task-id> --notes="Implementation complete. Production code verified."

workflow.name: Architect State Flow
workflow.description: Sequential planning phases 1-7. The architect captures requirements, writes proposals, coordinates review consensus, and hands off to supervisor.

action.instruction: Capture user request verbatim via /aura:user-request
Classify request along 4 axes: scope, complexity, risk, domain novelty

exit-condition.condition: Classification confirmed, research and explore complete

action.instruction: Run URE survey with user via /aura:user-elicit
Create URD as single source of truth for requirements
Write full technical proposal: interfaces, approach, validation checklist, BDD criteria
Create PROPOSAL-N task via /aura:architect:propose-plan
Spawn 3 axis-specific reviewers (A=Correctness, B=Test quality, C=Elegance)
Wait for all 3 reviewers to vote

exit-condition.condition: Any reviewer votes REVISE — create PROPOSAL-N+1, mark old as superseded, re-spawn reviewers

action.instruction: Present plan to user with demonstrative examples via /aura:user-uat
Add ratify label to accepted PROPOSAL-N
Mark all prior proposals aura:superseded
Create placeholder IMPL_PLAN task
Create handoff document with full inline provenance at .git/.aura/handoff/
Transfer to supervisor via /aura:architect:handoff

exit-condition.condition: Handoff document stored at .git/.aura/handoff/, supervisor notified

workflow.name: Layer Cake
workflow.description: TDD layer-by-layer implementation within a vertical slice. Worker implements types first, then tests (will fail), then production code to make tests pass.

action.instruction: Read slice task and identify required types
Define types, interfaces, and schemas (no deps) — only types for YOUR slice

exit-condition.condition: All required types defined; file imports without error

action.instruction: Write tests importing production code (CLI/API users will run) — tests WILL fail
Verify tests import actual production code, not test-only export

exit-condition.condition: Tests written and import production code; typecheck passes; tests fail (expected)

action.instruction: Implement production code to make Layer 2 tests pass
Wire with real dependencies (not mocks in production code)
Run tests — all Layer 2 tests must pass
Commit completed work
Notify supervisor of completion via bd comments add

exit-condition.condition: All tests pass; no TODO placeholders; real deps wired; production code path verified via code inspection

workflow.name: Ride the Wave
workflow.description: Coordinated Phase 8-10 execution pattern. The supervisor orchestrates the full cycle: plan slices, launch workers, spawn reviewers for per-slice review, workers fix, repeat max 3 cycles per slice.

action.instruction: Read RATIFIED_PLAN and URD via bd show
Spawn ephemeral Explore subagents (`subagent_type=Explore`) for scoped codebase queries — NOT standing teams
Use Explore findings to decompose into vertical slices with integration points
Create leaf tasks (L1/L2/L3) for every slice

exit-condition.condition: All slices created with leaf tasks, dependency-chained, assigned

action.instruction: Spawn workers as Agent tool subagents (`subagent_type: "general-purpose"`, `run_in_background: true`); use TeamCreate only for >=3 slices with shared integration points
Monitor worker progress via bd list and bd show
Supervisor commits at integration points (atomic commits) — commit small, integrate early and often

exit-condition.condition: All workers have notified completion via bd comments add

action.instruction: Spawn reviewers via Task tool for per-slice code review
Reviewers create severity groups (BLOCKER/IMPORTANT/MINOR) per slice
Create FOLLOWUP epic if any IMPORTANT/MINOR findings exist
Workers fix BLOCKERs and IMPORTANT findings

exit-condition.condition: All reviewers ACCEPT, no open BLOCKERs — proceed to Phase 11 UAT
BLOCKERs or IMPORTANTs remain, cycles < 3 per slice — workers fix, spawn new ephemeral reviewers
3 cycles exhausted, IMPORTANT remain — track in FOLLOWUP, proceed to Phase 11
3 cycles exhausted per slice, BLOCKERs remain — escalate to architect for re-planning

figure.title: Architect State Flow — Sequential Planning Phases 1-7
Layer Cake — TDD Parallelism Within Vertical Slices
Ride the Wave — Coordinated Phase 8-10 Execution
